content.text:
Based on my review, here is my assessment.

```json
{
  "approved": true,
  "feedback": "The structural refactor is complete, faithful, and behavior-preserving. All seven planned modules exist under src/app/ with exactly the exports the task specified: dom-builders.ts (buildTable, makeNavLink, makeParentRow, makeBrowseRow, makeActionButton, makeSearchRow, renderBreadcrumb), render-browse.ts (renderBrowse), render-search.ts (renderSearch), toolbar-handlers.ts (doSearch, handleUpload), render-orchestrator.ts (render + init(refs) owning renderToken/unsubscribe), and context.ts holding the shared refs/getters/setters (an option the task explicitly permitted). app.ts is now a slim entry that exports startApp, re-exports renderBrowse/renderSearch, and keeps the bootstrap guard — so src/app.test.ts's existing `import { startApp, renderBrowse, renderSearch } from './app'` resolves unmodified.\n\nBehavior preservation verified by line-by-line comparison against git HEAD:src/app.ts. Every helper maps 1:1 with only the indirection swaps the context pattern requires (resultsEl→getResults(), api→getApi(), statusEl→getStatus(), render()→rerender()). The render-hook indirection is sound: init() calls setRenderHook(render) BEFORE the initial render(), so action-button handlers (created only during render) always invoke the real render, and the default no-op hook is never reachable from a real click. The render-race token and listener-teardown logic moved intact into render-orchestrator.ts, preserving the task-20 fixes. The chosen context+hook pattern cleanly breaks the would-be cycle (orchestrator→render-browse→dom-builders→orchestrator) and is well documented in context.ts — a better solution than the 'lazy import' the task mentioned as an alternative.\n\nVerification results: `npx tsc --noEmit` exits 0 (clean type-check). `npm test` passes all 301 tests, including the 82 unmodified app.test.ts scenarios and 40 new characterization tests (src/app.characterization.test.ts) that pin exact DOM/attribute/lifecycle details. No unused imports/variables, no dead code, no debug statements, no leftover TODOs, and all new public functions carry docstrings. Code follows existing conventions (textContent/element creation for user strings, setAttribute for hrefs, .js import specifiers).\n\nOne environment note (NOT a code defect): `npm run build` fails at the copy-assets.mjs step because node_modules/bootstrap-icons/font/bootstrap-icons.css is absent in this worktree — a stale/incomplete node_modules state unrelated to the refactor. The TypeScript compilation half (`tsc`) succeeds; the failure is on a static-asset copy that has no connection to these changes and would fail identically on the base branch. No action needed in code.",
  "issues": []
}
```
